questions.question: Which fundamental digital logic gate produces a HIGH output (1) only when all of its inputs are HIGH (1)?
What is the primary benefit of employing pipelining in a CPU architecture?
options: To reduce the total power consumption of the processor.
To simplify the instruction set architecture (ISA).
To increase the instruction throughput (number of instructions completed per unit time).
To eliminate the need for a cache memory hierarchy.
To reduce the physical die area required for the CPU.

questions.question: In modern out-of-order execution processors, what is the primary purpose of 'register renaming'?
options: To reduce the latency of memory accesses by storing frequently used register values closer to the execution units.
To eliminate false data dependencies (Write-After-Write and Read-After-Write hazards) and enable more instruction-level parallelism.
To simplify the instruction decoding process by mapping logical registers to a fixed set of physical registers.
To improve the accuracy of branch prediction by providing more registers for speculative execution.
To reduce the physical die area required for the register file.

questions.question: Which standard protocol is widely used in digital ICs and PCBs for debugging, in-circuit programming, and boundary scan testing of internal logic via a serial interface?
In low-power digital IC design, what is the primary technique used to reduce dynamic power consumption by disabling the clock signal to inactive functional blocks?
